hints.Rectas: 'Rectas' is an adjective meaning 'straight' in the accusative plural
hints.semitas: 'semitas' is a noun meaning 'paths' in the accusative plural
hints.eius: 'eius' is a pronoun meaning 'his/of him' in the genitive

hints.Indicative: Indicative would be 'facitis' — 'facite' is the 2nd plural imperative
hints.Infinitive: Infinitive would be 'facere' — 'facite' is the imperative plural

hints.fero, ferre, tuli, latum: These are the parts of 'ferre' (to carry/bear)
hints.fio, fieri, factus sum: These are the parts of 'fieri' (to become) — the passive of 'facere'
hints.fugio, fugere, fugi, fugitum: These are the parts of 'fugere' (to flee)

hints.Nominative: Nominative plural would be 'semitae' — 'semitas' with -as is accusative plural
hints.Ablative: Ablative plural would be 'semitis' — 'semitas' is accusative
hints.Genitive: Genitive plural would be 'semitarum' — 'semitas' is accusative plural

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'semitae' ends in -ae
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'semitae' ends in -ae
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'semitae' ends in -ae

hints.Nominative: Nominative plural would be 'rectae' — 'Rectas' agrees with 'semitas' in the accusative
hints.Ablative: Ablative plural would be 'rectis' — 'Rectas' is accusative plural
hints.Genitive: Genitive plural would be 'rectarum' — 'Rectas' is accusative agreeing with 'semitas'

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'is/ea/id' — 'eius' is genitive singular
hints.Dative: Dative would be 'ei' — 'eius' is genitive (his paths)
hints.Accusative: Accusative would be 'eum/eam/id' — 'eius' is the genitive form
